channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Appeals court rejects HUD homelessness overhaul saying it would be "disastrous"
item.description: The federal housing agency wants to shift money away from permanent housing and toward programs that impose sobriety and other conditions. Advocates warn that would push many back into homelessness.
item.content:encoded:
The federal housing agency wants to shift money away from permanent housing and toward programs that impose sobriety and other conditions. Advocates warn that would push many back into homelessness.

(Image credit: Jae C. Hong)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden

item.title: Iran war adds more expense for profit-starved U.S. grain farmers
item.description: New federal data shows American farmers are planting millions fewer acres of corn and wheat in response to much higher fertilizer prices since the U.S. attacked Iran.
item.content:encoded: New federal data shows American farmers are planting millions fewer acres of corn and wheat in response to much higher fertilizer prices since the U.S. attacked Iran.
item.dc:creator: Kirk Siegler

item.title: Is the U.S. Navy ready to clear sea mines in the Persian Gulf?
item.description: Despite the danger of sea mines, experts say that mine clearing has received minimal attention and funding from the U.S. Navy — and it's often overshadowed by more high-profile weapons systems.
item.content:encoded:
Despite the danger of sea mines, experts say that mine clearing has received minimal attention and funding from the U.S. Navy — and it's often overshadowed by more high-profile weapons systems.

(Image credit: Suy Se)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: Supreme Court majority seems inclined to rule against Trump on birthright citizenship
item.description: The majority seemed skeptical of the Trump administration's argument on birthright citizenship, appearing ready to rule in favor of upholding automatic citizenship for babies born on U.S. soil.
item.content:encoded:
The majority seemed skeptical of the Trump administration's argument on birthright citizenship, appearing ready to rule in favor of upholding automatic citizenship for babies born on U.S. soil.

(Image credit: Kent Nishimura)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: A propaganda war on the National Mall pits Trump against satirical statues and posters
item.description: The Trump administration and its critics are waging a war of images on the National Mall like none before. The president's face stares down from federal buildings while statues and posters mock him below.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration and its critics are waging a war of images on the National Mall like none before. The president's face stares down from federal buildings while statues and posters mock him below.

(Image credit: Brendan Smialowski/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt

item.title: Federal judge rules DHS illegally stripped immigration status from thousands who entered through CBP One App
item.description: Tuesday's ruling reinstates the immigration status of those who came via CBP One and whose status was terminated.
item.content:encoded: Tuesday's ruling reinstates the immigration status of those who came via CBP One and whose status was terminated.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán

item.title: Trump's executive order on mail voting is set to face legal challenges
item.description: An executive order from President Trump seeks to create federal lists of eligible voters and instructs the Postal Service to send ballots only to approved voters. It faces certain legal challenges.
item.content:encoded: An executive order from President Trump seeks to create federal lists of eligible voters and instructs the Postal Service to send ballots only to approved voters. It faces certain legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang

item.title: Israel strikes Lebanon as Trump is set to deliver a speech on the Iran war
item.description: As Israel expands its invasion of Lebanon, the White House says President Trump will provide "an important update" to the nation on the Iran war.
item.content:encoded:
As Israel expands its invasion of Lebanon, the White House says President Trump will provide "an important update" to the nation on the Iran war.

(Image credit: Bilal Hussein)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Homesick in a foreign country, a teenager meets a lifelong friend
item.description: Holly Deiaco-Smith was feeling homesick while studying abroad in France when she was 19 years old. An encounter at the post office changed everything and led to a decades-long friendship.
item.content:encoded: Holly Deiaco-Smith was feeling homesick while studying abroad in France when she was 19 years old. An encounter at the post office changed everything and led to a decades-long friendship.
item.dc:creator: Autumn Barnes

item.title: Supreme Court considers a historic case about who is — and isn't — born a citizen
item.description: At issue is President Trump's challenge to a constitutional provision that has long been interpreted to guarantee American citizenship to every child born in the United States.
item.content:encoded:
At issue is President Trump's challenge to a constitutional provision that has long been interpreted to guarantee American citizenship to every child born in the United States.

(Image credit: Alex Wroblewski)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg
